description: Agent declared with elevated shell tools but no explicit permission boundary

finding.title: Agent has no tool permission boundary
finding.detail: Agent is declared without a 'tools' key specifying allowed/denied tools. No explicit permission boundary means it inherits the global default.
finding.remediation: Add an explicit 'tools' section to each agent definition with allowed and denied tool lists.

description: Agent model not in approved list

finding.title: Agent model detected
finding.detail: Review agent model assignment. Ensure expensive models are not assigned to high-frequency background agents.
finding.remediation: Document the purpose of each agent and review model appropriateness. Use cheap models for automation, powerful models for user-facing work.

finding.title: Agent safeBins contains shell interpreter
finding.detail: At least one agent allows bash/sh/zsh in tools.exec.safeBins. This can bypass allowlist security via shell command chaining.
finding.remediation: Remove bash/sh/zsh from safeBins and allow only tightly scoped binaries required for the agent role.

finding.title: Agent safeBins contains OS automation binary
finding.detail: At least one agent allows OS automation binaries (for example osascript/powershell) in safeBins. This expands host-control and secret-access surface.
finding.remediation: Remove automation binaries from safeBins unless explicitly required and gated by strict operator approval.

finding.title: Agent safeBins contains package manager
finding.detail: At least one agent allows package managers (pip/npm/yarn/pnpm/cargo) in safeBins, which increases supply-chain execution risk.
finding.remediation: Remove package managers from safeBins. Install dependencies through controlled CI or prebuilt images.

finding.title: Agent safeBins contains infrastructure CLI
finding.detail: At least one agent allows cloud/container CLIs (docker/aws/kubectl/gcloud) in safeBins, increasing blast radius if prompts are injected.
finding.remediation: Scope infra CLIs to dedicated maintenance agents only, with strict workspace limits and exec approval gating.

description: Agent workspace is broad and workspaceOnly is disabled

finding.title: Broad agent workspace without fs.workspaceOnly
finding.detail: At least one agent uses a broad workspace path outside ~/.openclaw/agents while fs.workspaceOnly is false. This weakens filesystem isolation.
finding.remediation: Set workspace under ~/.openclaw/agents/<id>/workspace and enable tools.fs.workspaceOnly=true.

description: High-power tools allowed without exec ask=always

finding.title: High-power agent tools enabled without execution approval
finding.detail: At least one agent has sessions_spawn/cron/browser enabled while tools.exec.ask is not set to always.
finding.remediation: Set tools.exec.ask=always for agents with high-power capabilities and reduce default sessions/cron exposure.
